name: python-hostlist

metadata.name: python-hostlist
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License (GPL)

documentation: The hostlist.py module knows how to expand and collect Slurm hostlist expressions.

modules.name: hostlist
documentation:
Handle hostlist expressions.

This module provides operations to expand and collect hostlist
expressions.

The hostlist expression syntax is the same as in several programs
developed at LLNL (https://computing.llnl.gov/linux/). However in
corner cases the behaviour of this module have not been compared for
compatibility with pdsh/dshbak/SLURM et al.
!/usr/bin/env python
Hostlist library

functions.name: hostlist.expand_hostlist
documentation:
Expand a hostlist expression string to a Python list.

Example: expand_hostlist("n[9-11],d[01-02]") ==> 
         ['n9', 'n10', 'n11', 'd01', 'd02']

Unless allow_duplicates is true, duplicates will be purged
from the results. If sort is true, the output will be sorted.
Hostlist expansion

functions.name: hostlist.expand_part
documentation: Expand a part (e.g. "x[1-2]y[1-3][1-3]") (no outer level commas).

functions.name: hostlist.expand_rangelist
documentation: Expand a rangelist (e.g. "1-10,14"), putting a prefix before.

functions.name: hostlist.expand_range
documentation: Expand a range (e.g. 1-10 or 14), putting a prefix before.

functions.name: hostlist.remove_duplicates
documentation: Remove duplicates from a list (but keep the order).

functions.name: hostlist.collect_hostlist
documentation:
Collect a hostlist string from a Python list of hosts.

We start grouping from the rightmost numerical part.
Duplicates are removed.

A bad hostname raises an exception (unless silently_discard_bad
is true causing the bad hostname to be silently discarded instead).
Hostlist collection

functions.name: hostlist.collect_hostlist_1
documentation:
Collect a hostlist string from a list of hosts (left+right).

The input is a list of tuples (left, right). The left part
is analyzed, while the right part is just passed along
(it can contain already collected range expressions).

functions.name: hostlist.format_range
documentation: Format a range from low to high inclusively, with a certain width.

functions.name: hostlist.numerically_sorted
documentation:
Sort a list of hosts numerically.

E.g. sorted order should be n1, n2, n10; not n1, n10, n2.
Sort a list of hosts numerically

functions.name: hostlist.numeric_sort_key
documentation:
Compose a sorting key to compare strings "numerically":

We split numerical (integer) and non-numerical parts into a list,
making sure that the numerical parts are converted to Python ints,
and then sort on the lists. Thus, if we sort x10y and x9z8, we will
compare ["x", 10, "y"] with ["x", 9, "x", "8"] and return x9z8
before x10y".

Python 3 complication: We cannot compare int and str, so while we can
compare x10y and x9z8, we cannot compare x10y and 9z8. Kludge: insert
a blank string first if the list would otherwise start with an integer.
This will give the same ordering as before, as integers seem to compare
smaller than strings in Python 2.

functions.name: hostlist.parse_slurm_tasks_per_node
documentation: Parse SLURM_TASKS_PER_NODE into a list of task numbers
Description from the SLURM sbatch man page:
Number of tasks to be initiated on each node. Values
are comma separated and in the same order as
SLURM_NODELIST.  If two or more consecutive nodes are
to have the same task count, that count is followed by
"(x#)" where "#" is the repetition count. For example,
"SLURM_TASKS_PER_NODE=2(x3),1" indicates that the first
three nodes will each execute three tasks and the
fourth node will execute one task.

classes.name: hostlist.BadHostlist
documentation: Exception used for error reporting to the caller
